message:
chore(beads): reopen issue br-1u8gy (ATC DecisionCore edge case tests)

Update .beads/issues.jsonl to reflect issue br-1u8gy ("ATC tests:
DecisionCore edge cases") being reopened:

- Status changed from "closed" to "in_progress"
- The "closed_at" field was removed (issue is no longer closed)
- The "updated_at" timestamp updated to 2026-03-15T15:16:24Z

The "close_reason" field is retained as historical context describing
the previous closure. This issue tracks adding 5 edge case unit tests
for the DecisionCore in the ATC (Air Traffic Controller) module:
partial likelihoods, two-action core, uniform posterior, negative
alpha clamping, and empty likelihoods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -443,7 +443,7 @@
 {"id":"br-1t0e","title":"Resources: file_reservations resource should be bounded (avoid glob explosion)","description":"## Problem
`resource://file_reservations/{slug}` currently expands glob patterns via filesystem enumeration and may run `git log` over many pathspecs. On large projects with broad patterns (e.g. `src/**`), this can take >60s and effectively wedges the MCP server (subsequent tool/resource calls time out).

## Objective
Make `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` reliably fast and non-blocking for large repos while keeping legacy output shape.

## Scope
- Avoid enumerating all filesystem matches for glob patterns.
- Compute git activity without expanding to thousands of explicit paths (use git pathspec/glob or prefix-based strategy).
- Bound work per pattern (time/size caps) and fail open by returning `null` activity timestamps when exceeding caps.
- Ensure cleanup logic (TTL expiry + stale release) cannot monopolize the server request thread.

## Tests
- Unit tests covering glob-heavy patterns (`src/**`, `crates/**`, `**/*.rs`) ensure the code path does not enumerate full repo trees (use a temp dir with many files to assert completes quickly).
- Regression test: resource handler completes within a small time budget under a synthetic repo with many files and a broad glob reservation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resource://file_reservations/...` returns within a bounded time on large repos (no tool server wedge).
2. Output shape remains compatible (fields present, timestamps may be null when capped).
3. Existing conformance tests still pass; add targeted tests for the new caps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:05.771837055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745259737Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745241343Z","close_reason":"Avoid glob explosion in resource://file_reservations: stop filesystem glob expansion; use git pathspec glob magic; add regression test; ran fmt+clippy+test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1t0e","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1td4","title":"T8.3: Integrate local bundle validation stage into verify-live pipeline","description":"## Objective
Integrate existing local bundle checks into the native verification pipeline before remote probing.

## Work
- Reuse/compose `share::deploy::validate_bundle` output.
- Normalize local-check output into verify-live report schema.
- Ensure local failures can short-circuit remote checks when configured.

## Deliverable
Unified local validation stage feeding native verify-live results.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:40.877231971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:25:26.213429438Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:25:26.213409281Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deploy","share","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1td4","depends_on_id":"br-y3sk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"br-1td4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verify-live orchestration implemented: VerifyLiveOptions, map_bundle_check, map_probe_result, build_remote_checks, build_security_checks, run_verify_live. 224 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:25:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-1u5so","title":"Fix random-audit reconstruction and robot rendering correctness bugs","description":"Random deep audit found correctness issues: (1) reconstruct trims message body and mutates archived content; (2) robot thread ack status marks partial acks as done; (3) robot message attachment parser drops/mislabels metadata. This bead tracks line-local fixes plus targeted tests.","notes":"Implemented: robot thread ack now reports partial/done correctly; robot message attachment parser now supports AttachmentMeta shape (bytes/media_type/path fallback) and added focused tests. Also fixed per-DB lexical bridge initialization so first-seen sqlite pools trigger backfill even when bridge already exists. Validation: rch exec cargo fmt --check PASS; targeted cargo test/check blocked by upstream /dp/asupersync compile errors (connect signature + cx.now).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"DustyCardinal","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:40:58.097570657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T06:36:23.217786121Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T06:36:23.217761475Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"br-1u5so","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RubyPrairie 2026-03-02: Fixed reconstruct body trimming and outbox ack_status rendering. Also found and fixed: trend arrows always-flat bug in reservations+projects screens, future-timestamp display in mail_ui, empty-list cursor in explorer/search/messages screens, pid_alive perf issue in storage, and ensure_archive path injection.","created_at":"2026-03-03T06:29:07Z"},{"id":203,"issue_id":"br-1u5so","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed bug 3 (auto type filter leak) + 4 regression tests + storage compile fix. Bugs 1-2 confirmed NOT bugs.","created_at":"2026-03-03T06:36:19Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-1u8gy","title":"ATC tests: DecisionCore edge cases","description":"# DecisionCore Edge Case Tests

## What
Add unit tests for edge cases not covered by the existing 16 decision core tests.

## Tests to Add (in atc.rs, mod tests)

1. **Partial likelihoods**: call update_posterior with likelihoods for only
   SOME states (not all 3). States not in the map should get likelihood 1.0
   (uninformative). Verify the posterior shifts correctly for the specified
   states while the unspecified state's probability changes only via
   normalization.

2. **Two-action core**: create a DecisionCore with only 2 actions. Verify
   runner_up_loss is the loss of the other action (not INFINITY). This
   tests the runner_up tracking when there are exactly 2 alternatives.

3. **Uniform posterior**: create a core with P(s) = 1/3 for all states.
   Verify choose_action picks the action with lowest expected loss under
   uniform belief.

4. **Negative alpha clamping**: pass alpha = -1.0 to DecisionCore::new.
   Verify it's clamped to 0.01 (minimum).

5. **Empty likelihoods**: call update_posterior(&[]). Verify posterior
   doesn't change (all states get default likelihood 1.0).

## Files
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/atc.rs (add to mod tests)

## Logging
Each test should include the posterior values in assertion messages
for debugging: format!("posterior: {posterior:?}")","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:10:39.214025606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:29:08.480660707Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T06:29:08.480187731Z","close_reason":"Implemented all 5 edge case tests: partial likelihoods, two-action core, uniform posterior, negative alpha clamping, empty likelihoods. All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-1u8gy","title":"ATC tests: DecisionCore edge cases","description":"# DecisionCore Edge Case Tests

## What
Add unit tests for edge cases not covered by the existing 16 decision core tests.

## Tests to Add (in atc.rs, mod tests)

1. **Partial likelihoods**: call update_posterior with likelihoods for only
   SOME states (not all 3). States not in the map should get likelihood 1.0
   (uninformative). Verify the posterior shifts correctly for the specified
   states while the unspecified state's probability changes only via
   normalization.

2. **Two-action core**: create a DecisionCore with only 2 actions. Verify
   runner_up_loss is the loss of the other action (not INFINITY). This
   tests the runner_up tracking when there are exactly 2 alternatives.

3. **Uniform posterior**: create a core with P(s) = 1/3 for all states.
   Verify choose_action picks the action with lowest expected loss under
   uniform belief.

4. **Negative alpha clamping**: pass alpha = -1.0 to DecisionCore::new.
   Verify it's clamped to 0.01 (minimum).

5. **Empty likelihoods**: call update_posterior(&[]). Verify posterior
   doesn't change (all states get default likelihood 1.0).

## Files
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/atc.rs (add to mod tests)

## Logging
Each test should include the posterior values in assertion messages
for debugging: format!("posterior: {posterior:?}")","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:10:39.214025606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:16:24.996280513Z","close_reason":"Implemented all 5 edge case tests: partial likelihoods, two-action core, uniform posterior, negative alpha clamping, empty likelihoods.  {"id":"br-1ubw","title":"renew_reservations read-modify-write without transaction","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:35:56.048700152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:50:08.957016306Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:50:08.956992922Z","close_reason":"Wrapped entire SELECT + UPDATE loop in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT transaction with rollback on error","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-1uf","title":"Share/Export Pipeline Parity (Static Bundle)","description":"## Objective
Implement the full **Share/Export pipeline** (static bundle) as specified in `EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md` (Share/Export section). This includes DB snapshotting, scrub presets, search indexes, viewer assets, bundle scaffolding, signing/encryption, and deterministic ZIP packaging.

## Scope (15 Steps)
1. create_sqlite_snapshot
2. apply_project_scope
3. scrub_snapshot (presets + secret regex)
4. build_search_indexes (FTS5)
5. build_materialized_views
6. create_performance_indexes
7. finalize_snapshot_for_export
8. bundle_attachments
9. maybe_chunk_database
10. copy_viewer_assets
11. export_viewer_data
12. write_bundle_scaffolding
13. sign_manifest
14. encrypt_bundle
15. package_directory_as_zip

## Tests
- Unit tests per step for deterministic behavior.
- Integration tests for end‑to‑end export/update flows.
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.4`.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Share pipeline artifacts under `tests/artifacts/share/<timestamp>/`.
- JSON summaries + diffs for each step.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All steps are implemented per spec with legacy parity.
2. Deterministic bundles (stable hashes) are enforced in tests.
3. E2E share/export suite passes with detailed artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"PearlOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:14:52.252531998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.785687923Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.767320259Z","close_reason":"All 15 share/export pipeline steps implemented and covered by deterministic/conformance tests; unblocking downstream CLI/e2e work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1uf","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Investigate cache invalidation/read-through race or stale state in inbox stats path and make test deterministic/pass in full suite.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T18:30:52.509461067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:37:33.223397949Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:37:33.223360088Z","close_reason":"Test passes - cache miss read-through and lru_evict_if_full_i64 both work correctly.
